subject: Bellwood Plaza Redevelopment - Introduction and Request for Pre-Application Meeting
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

My name is Marcus Trellis and I am the managing principal of Trellis Plaza Partners LLC. We have an accepted purchase contract on the former Bellwood Plaza retail site at the northeast corner of St. Charles Rd and 25th Ave (PIN 15-09-204-018 and the adjoining parcel), the roughly 3.1-acre property that has sat largely vacant since the grocery anchor closed in 2019.

We are proposing a transit-oriented mixed-use redevelopment: a single four-story building with 84 residential apartments above approximately 14,500 square feet of ground-floor retail fronting St. Charles Rd, structured around 126 surface and tuck-under parking spaces. Roughly 11 of the 84 units would be set aside as affordable at or below 60% of area median income. Total project cost is estimated at $41.6 million.

Because the site sits within the St. Charles Rd corridor and is walkable to the Metra UP-W station, we believe it is an ideal candidate for the kind of infill the Village has said it wants. To make the numbers work given environmental remediation of the old parking field and the cost of structured parking, we will be requesting tax increment financing assistance of approximately $3.2 million through a redevelopment agreement, reimbursed on a pay-as-you-go basis from the increment the project itself generates.

I would be grateful for a pre-application meeting with you, the Village Manager, and your building and engineering staff before we file formally. We are targeting the March Plan Commission cycle. Could your office suggest some dates in early February?

Thank you for your time and consideration.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Bellwood Plaza Redevelopment - Pre-Application Coordination
body_clean:
Mr. Trellis,

Thank you for reaching out, and for the level of detail in your introduction. The Bellwood Plaza corner has been a priority eyesore on St. Charles Rd for far too long, and the Village is genuinely interested in seeing quality reinvestment there. I want to be candid that a project of this scale, with a TIF request in the $3 million range, will draw close scrutiny from the Board and from the surrounding neighborhood, and rightly so.

I am asking Karen Whitfield, our Village Manager, to coordinate the pre-application meeting. Please come prepared to walk us through (1) your site plan and massing, including how the four-story height relates to the single-family homes on 25th Ave and the rear lot line, (2) a parking and traffic analysis, because the St. Charles and 25th intersection already backs up at the Metra peak, and (3) a preliminary sources-and-uses that justifies the gap you say the TIF would fill. We will expect an independent review of any but-for analysis.

Karen will also loop in Rosa Marchetti on the zoning and variance posture, since the current B-2 zoning will almost certainly require relief, and Tom Reyes, our village engineer, on traffic and stormwater. I would suggest we hold the working meeting before you finalize anything for the March Plan Commission cycle so there are no surprises at the public hearing.

We will follow up with dates this week. I look forward to it.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Zoning and Variance Analysis - Bellwood Plaza Mixed-Use
body_clean:
Mayor, following the February 6 pre-application meeting, here is my preliminary zoning read on the Trellis proposal at St. Charles Rd and 25th Ave so you have it before this goes public.

The site is zoned B-2 General Commercial. Multi-family residential above retail is not a permitted use by right in B-2, so the applicant will need either a map amendment to a mixed-use or planned development designation, or approval as a Planned Unit Development under Section 11-13 of the zoning code. I am recommending the PUD route because it lets us attach conditions.

Even as a PUD, the project requires several deviations from the underlying standards. The big ones: building height of 48 feet 6 inches against a 35-foot maximum; a rear yard setback of 12 feet where 25 feet is required abutting residential, which is the line shared with the single-family lots on 25th Ave; and parking. The applicant proposes 126 spaces. Our ordinance, applied straight, would require 1.5 spaces per dwelling unit plus retail parking, which works out to roughly 184 spaces. That is a 58-space shortfall, and they are leaning on the Metra proximity and a shared-parking study to justify it. I am skeptical of the retail parking assumptions and have asked for the shared-parking analysis in writing.

The height and the rear setback are what neighbors will react to most strongly. I would not want the Board to assume any of these deviations are routine. We should also flag that a traffic impact study is a condition I intend to require before the Plan Commission hearing, and I have copied Tom on that.

The applicant is targeting the Plan Commission for March 19. I can hold that date if the traffic study is in by March 5.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Traffic Impact Study Review - 25th Ave and St. Charles Rd
body_clean:
Mayor, Karen, I have completed my review of the traffic impact study the applicant's consultant (Kenig Lindgren / KLOA) submitted on March 4 for the Bellwood Plaza mixed-use project, and I want to give you my honest engineering assessment before the March 19 Plan Commission hearing.

The study uses ITE trip generation rates for mid-rise multifamily and small-format retail and projects 41 net new AM peak-hour trips and 58 PM peak-hour trips after a transit and pass-by reduction. On its face the intersection of St. Charles Rd and 25th Ave is shown remaining at Level of Service C in the AM and LOS D in the PM, which the consultant frames as acceptable.

My concerns are three. First, the pass-by reduction they apply to the retail (34%) is aggressive for this corridor and I would want it capped closer to 20%. Second, the study does not adequately address the Metra UP-W gate-down queuing at the 25th Ave crossing; during the morning gate-down, the southbound 25th Ave approach already spills back past Washington Blvd, and the study's queuing model assumes no gate events. Third, the proposed full-access driveway on 25th Ave sits only about 95 feet north of the St. Charles intersection, which is short for stacking and creates a left-turn conflict.

None of this is fatal. I think the project can work with a right-in/right-out restriction on the 25th Ave drive, a contribution toward signal timing optimization at St. Charles and 25th, and a revised parking study. But I cannot tell the Plan Commission the traffic is a non-issue, and I will say so on the record if asked.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Strong Opposition to the 84-Apartment Project at 25th and St. Charles
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I have lived in my home on the 200 block of 25th Ave for 37 years, directly behind the old Bellwood Plaza, and I am writing to register my firm opposition to the Trellis apartment project before the Plan Commission hearing on March 19.

I understand the Village wants that corner cleaned up. So do I. But what is being proposed is a four-and-a-half-story building, nearly 49 feet tall, looming over single-family homes that are one and two stories. The plans show the building sitting just 12 feet off my back lot line where the code requires 25. That is not infill, that is a wall. My neighbors and I will lose light, privacy, and the open sky we have looked at for decades, and our property values will follow.

Then there is the traffic. Anyone who lives here knows what 25th Ave is like when the Metra gates come down in the morning. We are already backed up to Washington Blvd. Adding 84 apartments, plus retail customers, plus a driveway dumping cars onto 25th Ave a hundred feet from the light, is going to make a dangerous situation worse. Children walk to school across that intersection.

And I object to my tax dollars subsidizing this. $3.2 million in TIF for an out-of-town developer to build luxury apartments? If the project cannot stand on its own, maybe it is the wrong project. The increment that gets diverted is increment our schools and the library do not see for the life of the district.

I am asking you to vote no, or at minimum to insist the building be brought down in height and pulled back from our homes. I and at least a dozen neighbors intend to speak on the 19th.

subject: Concerns from Route 64 Sports Bar on Parking and Corridor Impact
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo,

I own Route 64 Sports Bar at 540 St. Charles Rd, four doors east of the old Bellwood Plaza, and I want to weigh in on the Trellis mixed-use proposal as a business owner who has hung on through some lean years on this corridor.

I will start by saying I am not flatly against it. Frankly, getting that dead plaza filled with 84 households and ground-floor shops could be the best thing to happen to St. Charles Rd in a decade. Foot traffic is what businesses like mine and El Faro down the block live and die on, and right now after about 8 p.m. that stretch is dark and dead because of the vacant lot.

My real concern is parking, and I want it on the record before March 19. We already compete for street parking on St. Charles between 25th and 50th Ave on weekend nights and during anything at the Metra lot. The developer is asking to build 58 fewer parking spaces than the code requires, betting that apartment residents will not own cars because of the train. With respect, that is a fantasy in Bellwood. Those residents will own cars, and they will park them on St. Charles Rd in front of my door and El Faro's. On a Saturday when there's a fight on or a Bears game, my customers already circle the block.

If the Village is going to grant a 58-space parking variance, I am asking that you condition it on (1) a residential permit-parking restriction on the side streets so apartment overflow does not eat 25th Ave and the 19th Ave blocks, and (2) some shared-parking arrangement that the evening retail and restaurant uses can actually use after business hours. Get the parking right and I will support this at the podium.

subject: In Support of the Bellwood Plaza Redevelopment
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo,

I am a Bellwood resident and homeowner on Marshall Ave, and I want to add a voice in favor of the Trellis mixed-use project at St. Charles Rd and 25th Ave, because I suspect the March 19 hearing will be dominated by the people against it.

That plaza has been a vacant, fenced eyesore since the grocery store closed in 2019. It collects litter, the parking field floods, and it does nothing for our tax base or our corridor. Six years of nothing. Meanwhile younger families and seniors looking to downsize have almost no decent newer rental options in this village, and the ones we have are aging. Eighty-four units, with eleven of them affordable, walkable to the UP-W train, is exactly the kind of project a transit suburb should want.

I hear the traffic and parking worries and I think they are worth conditioning the approval on, not killing it over. People said the same things about every apartment building ever proposed near a train station, and the towns that built them are thriving while the towns that blocked them are stuck with empty lots. A four-story building on a major state-route corridor is not out of scale; St. Charles Rd is not a residential street.

On the TIF, I would just ask that the Board hold the developer to a real but-for test and a pay-as-you-go structure so the Village is not writing a check up front. If the increment pays it back, $3.2 million to turn a six-year hole in the ground into housing, shops, and a much bigger tax base later seems like a reasonable deal for Bellwood. I support it and I will say so on the 19th.

subject: FOIA Request - Bellwood Plaza Redevelopment Agreement and TIF Records
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo and Clerk Pierce,

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, 5 ILCS 140, I am requesting copies of the following public records concerning the proposed mixed-use redevelopment of the former Bellwood Plaza at St. Charles Rd and 25th Ave by Trellis Plaza Partners LLC:

1. Any draft or executed redevelopment agreement between the Village and Trellis Plaza Partners LLC, including all term sheets and exhibits.
2. All records reflecting the requested TIF assistance of approximately $3.2 million, including the developer's sources-and-uses statement, the pro forma submitted in support of the but-for finding, and any independent third-party review of that pro forma the Village commissioned.
3. The traffic impact study dated on or about March 4, 2025 and any staff review or correspondence concerning it.
4. The shared-parking analysis referenced in the building official's zoning memo.
5. All written communications, including emails, between any Village official or employee and any representative of Trellis Plaza Partners LLC from December 1, 2024 to the present.

I am requesting these as electronic copies. I am not requesting them on a commercial basis. If any portion is withheld or redacted, please cite the specific exemption under Section 7 of the Act. I understand the Village has five business days to respond and may extend by an additional five.

I am submitting this before the March 19 Plan Commission hearing because residents are being asked to comment on a deal whose financial terms have not been made public. Transparency on a $3.2 million public subsidy is not too much to ask.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Response to FOIA Request - Bellwood Plaza Redevelopment Records
body_clean:
Mr. Carter,

This acknowledges receipt of your FOIA request dated March 12, 2025 regarding the proposed Bellwood Plaza redevelopment by Trellis Plaza Partners LLC. I have logged it as FOIA 2025-047 and it was received in this office at 9:18 a.m. on March 12, so the Village's five-business-day response is due March 19.

I want to be straightforward about what we can and cannot produce in that window. Items 3 and 4, the March 4 traffic impact study and the shared-parking analysis, are responsive public records and I expect to release them by the deadline, along with the staff review memos from Mr. Reyes and Ms. Marchetti.

Item 1 is more limited than you may expect. There is at present no executed redevelopment agreement. What exists is a draft term sheet that remains in active negotiation. Draft agreements that are still being negotiated may be exempt as predecisional and deliberative under Section 7(1)(f) of the Act, and portions reflecting the developer's private financial information may be exempt under 7(1)(g). I am reviewing item 2, the pro forma and sources-and-uses, with the same considerations; proprietary financial data a developer submits is frequently exempt, though the independent third-party review may be more disclosable.

Item 5 is broad. Capturing all emails from December 1 forward across multiple officials will likely require the five-business-day extension permitted under Section 3(e), and I am notifying you now that I intend to invoke it for that item only, with a revised due date of March 26.

I will release everything I can by March 19 and provide an exemption log for anything withheld. Nothing in this response is meant to delay your participation in the hearing.

subject: El Faro Cantina - Construction Impact and Corridor Questions
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I am writing on behalf of El Faro Cantina at 612 St. Charles Rd. We have run our family restaurant on this corridor for nine years, three doors from the old Bellwood Plaza, and I want to share where we stand on the Trellis project ahead of the March 19 hearing.

Like Route 64, we are cautiously in favor. A filled corner with 84 households within a two-minute walk of our door would be a real boost; right now the dead plaza next to us makes the whole block feel unsafe after dark and we lose dinner business because of it. We want this to succeed.

What worries us is the construction period, not the finished building. A $41 million, four-story project is going to mean 18 to 24 months of demolition, excavation, and remediation of that contaminated parking field right beside us. During the El Faro and Route 64 dinner hours, we cannot survive lane closures on St. Charles Rd, loss of the on-street parking in front of our businesses, dust, and detoured customers. We lived through the 25th Ave water main work two summers ago and it nearly closed us.

So my questions for the Village, which I hope can be answered or conditioned at the hearing: Will there be a construction management plan that protects access and on-street parking for the existing St. Charles Rd businesses? Can heavy hauling and lane closures be restricted to off-peak and non-dinner hours? Will the developer be required to maintain pedestrian access and signage directing customers to us during the work? And is there any small-business impact mitigation in the redevelopment agreement?

We are not asking you to stop progress. We are asking you not to let the cure kill the patients next door.

subject: Pre-Hearing Briefing and Recommended Conditions - Bellwood Plaza
body_clean:
Mayor, here is my consolidated briefing ahead of the March 19 Plan Commission hearing on the Trellis Plaza Partners mixed-use proposal, pulling together where staff, residents, and corridor businesses have landed.

Where we are: Tom's traffic review (seq 4) is workable but conditional. Rosa's zoning memo (seq 3) confirms this needs a PUD with deviations for height (48'6" vs 35'), rear setback (12' vs 25' against the 25th Ave homes), and a 58-space parking variance. David's office still has not received a defensible but-for pro forma; the $3.2M TIF ask cannot be recommended until we have an independent review, and I would not let the Board take final action on the redevelopment agreement on the 19th regardless. The Plan Commission can make a recommendation on zoning; the RDA and TIF come back to the Village Board separately.

The public split is real. We have organized opposition from the 25th Ave neighbors (Ms. Brzezinski, seq 5) focused on height, setback, and the TIF; corridor businesses Route 64 and El Faro are conditionally supportive but anxious about parking overflow and construction impacts; and we have written support (Mr. Ashford, seq 7). Carter's FOIA (seq 8) is live and Yolanda is releasing the studies on the 19th.

My recommendation is to advance the project to the Plan Commission with these conditions on the table: reduce height to four stories / 42 feet and pull the rear setback to at least 18 feet; right-in/right-out only on the 25th Ave drive plus a signal-timing contribution; residential permit parking on 25th, 19th, and the adjacent side streets; a binding construction management plan protecting St. Charles Rd business access; and a pay-as-you-go TIF capped at $3.2M contingent on an independent but-for finding. I would not commit to the dollar figure publicly yet.

Happy to walk through this before Tuesday.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave

subject: Direction Heading Into the March 19 Plan Commission Hearing
body_clean:
Karen, thank you, and to Rosa, Tom, David, and Yolanda for the work pulling this together on a tight timeline. Here is my direction heading into Tuesday.

I want the corner redeveloped. Six years of a fenced, flooding eyesore on St. Charles Rd is not acceptable, and the support we are hearing from Mr. Ashford and, conditionally, from Route 64 and El Faro reflects a real appetite for reinvestment. But I am not going to rubber-stamp a 49-foot building 12 feet off Eleanor Brzezinski's back lot line, and I am not going to commit a $3.2 million public subsidy without an independent but-for review in hand. Those two things are non-negotiable for me.

So for the 19th: present this as advancing with conditions, not as a finished deal. Adopt Karen's condition list as the staff recommendation, with the height brought down toward 42 feet and the rear setback pulled back to protect the 25th Ave homes, right-in/right-out on the 25th Ave drive with a signal contribution, residential permit parking on the side streets to answer Route 64's and El Faro's overflow concern, and a binding construction management plan that protects access and on-street parking for the existing corridor businesses during demolition and remediation. Make clear from the dais that the Plan Commission is making a zoning recommendation only and that the redevelopment agreement and the TIF come back to the Village Board on their own track, after David's independent review.

Yolanda, release everything you can on the studies under Carter's FOIA on the 19th so no one can say we negotiated a public subsidy in the dark; we will defend the deliberative exemptions on the draft term sheet honestly.

Let's be transparent, let's listen Tuesday night, and let's get the conditions right. I'll be there.
address_hint: Bellwood Plaza, St. Charles Rd & 25th Ave
